message:
fix(pager): fresh-eyes review cleanups on pager test fixes

- test_stale_larger_commit_publish_does_not_reopen_newer_shrunken_snapshot:
  drop a redundant publish_insert_single(page_two) after the truncate —
  publish_truncate_checkpoint's `pages.retain(|p| p <= max_page)` already
  keeps page_two (2 <= 4) and only removes page_one explicitly. The
  previous comment misstated that the truncate "swept every page".
- test_dirty_bitmap_{double_write,commit_flushes_all}: reword the
  "why the assertion is 0" comments to spell out the exact Page-1 write
  gate (`requires_page_one_rewrite() || requires_page_count_advance()`)
  and explicitly note that these tests exercise pre-seeded pages with
  no db_growth, so neither gate fires. The earlier comment incorrectly
  implied db_growth never emits a Page-1 frame.
- test_dirty_bitmap_double_write_dedups_wal_entry: revert a spurious
  log-count change that conflated `already_dirty_skip_count` (the
  dedup-hits counter, correctly 1 for a real double-write) with the
  Page-1 metadata frame count. The diagnostic now reads truthfully.

Verified: 11 targeted tests pass (8 dirty_bitmap, 2 publish_commit tests,
memory_db_allocator).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
